message:
bd-3qvi: close ftui migration epic — all children complete

All 6 blocking beads resolved:
bd-342l, bd-1ujr, bd-2jlf, bd-hak3, bd-36a2, bd-1zkm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -67,7 +67,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3nxm","title":"Add inline mode support for CLI+TUI coexistence","description":"# Add Inline Mode Support

## What
Leverage ftui's unique inline mode capability to provide a CLI+TUI hybrid experience.
Instead of taking over the full terminal (alt-screen), the TUI renders at the bottom
N rows while scan progress and log output scroll above.

## Why This Matters
Currently, the TUI takes over the full terminal (alt-screen mode). When you quit, all
TUI state disappears. With inline mode:
- Scan progress messages scroll above the TUI
- Log output (warnings, errors) is visible during TUI interaction
- Terminal scrollback preserves history after TUI exits
- Users can scroll up to see scan output while browsing processes

## ftui Inline Mode
```rust
let config = ProgramConfig {
    screen_mode: ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 20 },
    // ...
};
```

- `ui_height`: Fixed number of rows for the TUI at bottom of terminal
- Above the TUI: normal scrollback region for logs and output
- `Cmd::log("message")` writes to the scrollback region above
- Auto-remeasure: ftui can adjust ui_height based on content

## Implementation
1. Add `--inline` flag to `run --interactive` command
2. Default to alt-screen (current behavior) for backwards compatibility
3. When `--inline`, use `ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: computed }`
4. Compute ui_height based on terminal height (e.g., terminal_height - 5 for scroll area)
5. Scan progress output uses `Cmd::log()` to appear in scroll area
6. Detail pane may need to be more compact in inline mode

## Integration with Progress Emitter
pt-core has a `ProgressEmitter` for scan progress. In inline mode, connect it to
`Cmd::log()` so scan progress appears in the scrollback above the TUI.

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/main.rs` — add --inline flag, configure ScreenMode
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — handle inline-specific layout constraints

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--inline` flag enables inline mode
- Default (no flag) uses alt-screen (unchanged behavior)
- Scan progress scrolls above TUI in inline mode
- TUI renders at bottom of terminal
- Terminal scrollback preserved after exit
- Layout adapts to inline constraints","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:53.915574064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:21:16.147201238Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:21:16.147123153Z","close_reason":"Implemented --inline TUI mode; Cmd::log progress; docs + test gating fixes","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enhancement","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nxm","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:22:23.276808294Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nxm","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:53.971170573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3p52","title":"Port ProcessDetail widget (3 sub-views) to ftui","description":"# Port ProcessDetail Widget to ftui (with Evidence Ledger)

## What
Migrate the ProcessDetail widget (tui/widgets/process_detail.rs, 716 lines) which renders
a multi-view detail pane with 3 sub-views: Summary, Genealogy, and Galaxy Brain.

This bead also incorporates the evidence ledger display from the Phase 7 UX requirements
(process_triage-2ka). The evidence ledger provides an exportable, scrollable audit trail
of all Bayesian evidence contributions — crucial for the "explain decisions" goal.

---

## Current ProcessDetail Structure

### DetailView enum
```rust
pub enum DetailView {
    Summary,     // Process info, classification, confidence, evidence
    Genealogy,   // Process family tree (parent/child relationships)
    GalaxyBrain, // Full mathematical analysis (posterior distributions)
}
```

### Summary View Content
- Process classification (KILL/REVIEW/SPARE) with colored badge
- Confidence level (e.g., "High (0.92)")
- Why summary (1-2 sentence explanation)
- Top evidence lines (bullet list)
- Execution plan preview (if selected)

### Genealogy View Content
- Parent process info
- Child process list
- Process tree visualization

### Galaxy Brain View Content
- Full posterior distribution breakdown
- Log odds for each classification
- Evidence contribution per feature
- Mathematical trace of Bayesian inference
- Rendered as scrollable styled text with color-coded numbers

---

## ftui Widget Mapping

### For Summary View
- Use ftui `Block` with title for the container
- Use ftui `Paragraph` for text sections
- Use ftui `Badge` for classification label (KILL/REVIEW/SPARE)
- Use ftui `List` for evidence bullet points
- Vertical `Flex` layout to stack sections

### For Genealogy View
- Use ftui `Tree` widget for hierarchical process tree
- This is a major upgrade — current code draws the tree manually with ASCII art
- ftui Tree supports expand/collapse, lazy loading, custom item rendering

### For Galaxy Brain View
- Use ftui `Paragraph` with rich styled text (Spans/Lines)
- Use ftui `Scrollbar` for scrolling through long analysis
- Color-coded numbers: positive log-odds green, negative red
- Could potentially use ftui `JsonView` for structured data display

---

## Evidence Ledger (from Phase 7 UX requirements)

The Galaxy Brain sub-view should include or link to an evidence ledger that provides:
- Complete audit trail of Bayesian evidence contributions
- Each evidence item: feature name, observed value, log Bayes factor, direction
- Sortable by impact (absolute log Bayes factor)
- Exportable to file (`e` key in Galaxy Brain view → save to JSONL)

### Evidence Ledger Display
```
Evidence Ledger for PID 12345 (rsyslogd)
═══════════════════════════════════════
 #  Feature         Observed  logBF   Direction
 1  cpu_fraction    0.02      +3.21   → spare
 2  runtime_sec     86400     +2.44   → useful
 3  has_tty         false     -1.87   → abandoned
 4  orphan          false     +1.52   → useful
 5  net_active      true      +0.98   → useful
 6  io_active       false     -0.67   → abandoned
 7  command_cat     daemon    +0.45   → useful
 8  state_flag      Sleeping  +0.12   → useful
───────────────────────────────────────
    Net logBF: +6.18  →  Classification: SPARE (p=0.94)
```

### Export to File
When user presses `x` (export) in Galaxy Brain view:
```rust
Msg::ExportEvidenceLedger => {
    let pid = self.selected_process_pid();
    let ledger = self.build_evidence_ledger(pid);
    Cmd::perform(
        async move { save_ledger_to_file(ledger).await },
        Msg::LedgerExported,
    )
}
```

---

## Tab Bar for Sub-Views
Use ftui's `Tabs` widget to show [Summary | Genealogy | Galaxy Brain] tabs at the top
of the detail pane. This provides clear visual affordance for view switching:
```rust
let tabs = Tabs::new(vec\!["Summary", "Genealogy", "Galaxy Brain"])
    .select(self.active_detail_view as usize)
    .style(sheet.get("tabs.inactive"))
    .highlight_style(sheet.get("tabs.active"));
frame.render_widget(tabs, tabs_area);
```
Current code just switches content without tab indicators — this is a UX improvement.

---

## View Switching
In ftui Elm architecture, `DetailView` is part of Model state. The `s`/`t`/`g` keys
produce `Msg::SetDetailView(DetailView::Summary)` etc. The `view()` function checks
which sub-view to render and dispatches accordingly.

---

## Unit Tests (~8)
```rust
#[test]
fn summary_view_shows_classification_badge() { ... }
#[test]
fn genealogy_tree_shows_parent_and_children() { ... }
#[test]
fn galaxy_brain_renders_log_odds_with_colors() { ... }
#[test]
fn evidence_ledger_sorts_by_impact() { ... }
#[test]
fn tab_indicator_shows_active_view() { ... }
#[test]
fn view_switch_updates_content() { ... }
#[test]
fn detail_updates_on_selection_change() { ... }
#[test]
fn scrolling_works_in_galaxy_brain() { ... }
```

---

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/widgets/process_detail.rs` — rewrite using ftui widgets
- Consider splitting into sub-modules if the file gets large

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summary view shows classification badge, confidence, evidence, plan preview
- Genealogy view uses ftui Tree for process hierarchy (expand/collapse)
- Galaxy Brain view renders scrollable mathematical analysis with colored numbers
- Evidence ledger display with sortable impact column
- Export evidence ledger to JSONL file via `x` key
- Tab indicator shows active sub-view ([Summary | Genealogy | Galaxy Brain])
- View switches via s/t/g keys
- Content updates when selected process changes
- Scrolling works within detail pane (for long Galaxy Brain + ledger output)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:10:10.949503718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:37:18.296578992Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:37:18.296498553Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tui","widget"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-2hmx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:18:08.498895407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:59.793168982Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:10:13.257239058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-3w07","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:18:16.527134335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3p7w","title":"E2E coverage remaining surfaces (shadow/diff/telemetry/config/bundle-enc)","description":"## Goal
Fill remaining end-to-end integration coverage gaps with rich JSONL logging + artifacts.

## Scope (Child Tasks)
- Shadow mode lifecycle (start/run/stop/export/report).
- Session diff/compare workflows (pt diff + agent diff).
- Telemetry maintenance (status/prune/retention).
- Config preset workflows (list/show/diff/export/validate).
- Bundle encryption/verification flows (passphrase env + CLI).
- Output formatting modes (fields/compact/TOON/truncation).
- Capabilities manifest + cache flows (agent capabilities).
- Supervision gating + protected patterns (plan/action guardrails).
- Policy guardrails (rate limits, blast radius, max kills).
- Degraded environment + permissions (missing deps, permission errors, unsupported features).

## Logging & Artifacts
- All E2E scripts emit JSONL logs with shared schema + artifact manifests.
- Failure-mode coverage required (invalid inputs, missing files, wrong passphrase, malformed policy).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each listed surface has deterministic E2E coverage in CI.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate for every script.
- [ ] Negative-paths are explicitly validated for each surface.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:59:45.324582665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T04:19:10.861979405Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T04:19:10.861845837Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p7w","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:59:45.354597129Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3qvi","title":"EPIC: Migrate process_triage TUI from ratatui/crossterm to FrankenTUI","description":"# EPIC: Migrate process_triage TUI to FrankenTUI

## Background & Motivation

process_triage currently has a ~5,100-line interactive TUI built on ratatui 0.29 + crossterm 0.28
(feature-gated behind `ui` in pt-core's Cargo.toml). The TUI provides process browsing, selection,
search/filter, detail views (summary/genealogy/galaxy-brain), confirmation dialogs, responsive
layout with 4 breakpoints, and session integration for audit trails. It works but is built on an
imperative event loop with direct terminal manipulation.

FrankenTUI (ftui, at /dp/frankentui) is our in-house terminal UI kernel that provides:
- Elm/Bubbletea architecture (Model trait with init/update/view/subscriptions)
- Deterministic, flicker-free rendering via buffer diffs and sync brackets
- 37+ production widgets (Table, TextInput, Modal, Tree, CommandPalette, etc.)
- Inline mode (UI at bottom, logs scroll above)
- Bayesian decision-making for diff strategy, resize coalescing, fuzzy search
- Constraint-based Flex/Grid layout with intrinsic sizing
- Comprehensive theme/style system with WCAG contrast checking
- Type-safe one-writer rule and RAII terminal cleanup
- Formal correctness proofs for rendering pipeline

Migrating to ftui gives us: better architecture (Elm vs imperative), flicker-free rendering,
inline mode for CLI+TUI coexistence, access to advanced widgets (CommandPalette, Tree, Toast,
VirtualizedList), and alignment with our broader tooling ecosystem.

## Scope

- Replace ratatui + crossterm with ftui as TUI dependency
- Rewrite TUI using ftui's Model trait (Elm architecture)
- Port all 7 widgets (process_table, process_detail, search_input, confirm_dialog,
  config_editor, help overlay, status bar)
- Port responsive layout system (4 breakpoints: wide/standard/compact/minimal)
- Port 3 themes (Dark/Light/HighContrast)
- Port all key bindings and event handling
- Port integration with pt-core data flow (scan → display → select → execute)
- Update all TUI tests (golden, integration, 68 E2E workflow tests)

## Current TUI File Inventory

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| tui/app.rs | 1,004 | App state, event loop, rendering |
| tui/events.rs | 203 | Key bindings, event dispatch |
| tui/layout.rs | 703 | Responsive layout, 4 breakpoints |
| tui/theme.rs | 212 | 3 themes, classification colors |
| tui/mod.rs | 58 | Module exports |
| tui/widgets/process_table.rs | 1,013 | Main table, sorting, selection |
| tui/widgets/process_detail.rs | 716 | Detail pane, 3 sub-views |
| tui/widgets/search_input.rs | 345 | Text input with history |
| tui/widgets/confirm_dialog.rs | 361 | Modal confirmation |
| tui/widgets/config_editor.rs | 478 | Config form editor |
| tui/widgets/mod.rs | 23 | Widget module exports |
| **Total** | **5,116** | |

## Architecture Shift

**Before (ratatui/crossterm):**
```
crossterm::event::read() → match Event → mutate App fields → terminal.draw(|f| app.render(f))
```

**After (ftui Elm):**
```
ftui Event → From<Event> for Msg → Model::update(msg) → Cmd → Model::view(frame) → BufferDiff → ANSI
```

## ftui Dependency Path
```toml
ftui = { path = "/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui" }
```

## Phases
1. Foundation (deps, Model skeleton, Message enum, runtime entry)
2. Theme & Style (port 3 themes to ftui StyleSheet/Theme)
3. Layout (responsive breakpoints via ftui Flex)
4. Widget migration (7 widgets)
5. App state & events (selection, sorting, search, focus, keybindings)
6. Integration (scan → display → execute pipeline)
7. Testing (golden, integration, 68 E2E workflows)
8. Cleanup (remove old deps, documentation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:05:49.827721481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:05:49.835820400Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3qvi","depends_on_id":"process_triage-h89","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:05:49.835767241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3qvi","title":"EPIC: Migrate process_triage TUI from ratatui/crossterm to FrankenTUI","description":"# EPIC: Migrate process_triage TUI to FrankenTUI

## Background & Motivation

process_triage currently has a ~5,100-line interactive TUI built on ratatui 0.29 + crossterm 0.28
(feature-gated behind `ui` in pt-core's Cargo.toml). The TUI provides process browsing, selection,
search/filter, detail views (summary/genealogy/galaxy-brain), confirmation dialogs, responsive
layout with 4 breakpoints, and session integration for audit trails. It works but is built on an
imperative event loop with direct terminal manipulation.

FrankenTUI (ftui, at /dp/frankentui) is our in-house terminal UI kernel that provides:
- Elm/Bubbletea architecture (Model trait with init/update/view/subscriptions)
- Deterministic, flicker-free rendering via buffer diffs and sync brackets
- 37+ production widgets (Table, TextInput, Modal, Tree, CommandPalette, etc.)
- Inline mode (UI at bottom, logs scroll above)
- Bayesian decision-making for diff strategy, resize coalescing, fuzzy search
- Constraint-based Flex/Grid layout with intrinsic sizing
- Comprehensive theme/style system with WCAG contrast checking
- Type-safe one-writer rule and RAII terminal cleanup
- Formal correctness proofs for rendering pipeline

Migrating to ftui gives us: better architecture (Elm vs imperative), flicker-free rendering,
inline mode for CLI+TUI coexistence, access to advanced widgets (CommandPalette, Tree, Toast,
VirtualizedList), and alignment with our broader tooling ecosystem.

## Scope

- Replace ratatui + crossterm with ftui as TUI dependency
- Rewrite TUI using ftui's Model trait (Elm architecture)
- Port all 7 widgets (process_table, process_detail, search_input, confirm_dialog,
  config_editor, help overlay, status bar)
- Port responsive layout system (4 breakpoints: wide/standard/compact/minimal)
- Port 3 themes (Dark/Light/HighContrast)
- Port all key bindings and event handling
- Port integration with pt-core data flow (scan → display → select → execute)
- Update all TUI tests (golden, integration, 68 E2E workflow tests)

## Current TUI File Inventory

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| tui/app.rs | 1,004 | App state, event loop, rendering |
| tui/events.rs | 203 | Key bindings, event dispatch |
| tui/layout.rs | 703 | Responsive layout, 4 breakpoints |
| tui/theme.rs | 212 | 3 themes, classification colors |
| tui/mod.rs | 58 | Module exports |
| tui/widgets/process_table.rs | 1,013 | Main table, sorting, selection |
| tui/widgets/process_detail.rs | 716 | Detail pane, 3 sub-views |
| tui/widgets/search_input.rs | 345 | Text input with history |
| tui/widgets/confirm_dialog.rs | 361 | Modal confirmation |
| tui/widgets/config_editor.rs | 478 | Config form editor |
| tui/widgets/mod.rs | 23 | Widget module exports |
| **Total** | **5,116** | |

## Architecture Shift

**Before (ratatui/crossterm):**
```
crossterm::event::read() → match Event → mutate App fields → terminal.draw(|f| app.render(f))
```

**After (ftui Elm):**
```
ftui Event → From<Event> for Msg → Model::update(msg) → Cmd → Model::view(frame) → BufferDiff → ANSI
```

## ftui Dependency Path
```toml
ftui = { path = "/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui" }
```

## Phases
1. Foundation (deps, Model skeleton, Message enum, runtime entry)
2. Theme & Style (port 3 themes to ftui StyleSheet/Theme)
3. Layout (responsive breakpoints via ftui Flex)
4. Widget migration (7 widgets)
5. App state & events (selection, sorting, search, focus, keybindings)
6. Integration (scan → display → execute pipeline)
7. Testing (golden, integration, 68 E2E workflows)
8.  {"id":"bd-3r6h","title":"Add unit tests for TUI key binding helpers","description":"Add focused unit tests for KeyBindings + handle_event mapping in crates/pt-core/src/tui/events.rs (ftui).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:45:59.762092954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:46:13.576344716Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:46:13.576279856Z","close_reason":"Added KeyBindings + handle_event unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["test","tui"]}
 {"id":"bd-3t22","title":"E2E: capabilities manifest + cache","description":"## Goal
Validate agent capabilities manifest generation + cache behavior end-to-end.

## Scope
- `pt agent capabilities --format json` schema validation.
- Cache hit/miss behavior (first run vs second run) + TTL/refresh behavior.
- Negative paths: unreadable cache dir, invalid cache contents, missing manifest.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing errors are clear and actionable.
- Consume fixtures produced by bd-1591 + bd-z3ma.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, cache_hit, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: raw outputs, schema validation reports, stderr snapshots, manifest.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Capabilities output is schema-valid and deterministic.
- [ ] Cache behavior validated across runs (hit/miss/refresh).
- [ ] Error paths produce clear messages + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script via shared harness with fixtures.
